rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive hydrocoupling of chiral 3-trans-cinnamoyloxazolidinones to give cyclized hydrodimers (trans-3,4-diaryl cyclopentanones) with diastereo- and enantiocontrol governed by supporting electrolyte.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of the α,β-unsaturated cinnamoyloxazolidinone to a Z-enolate-type anion radical, which dimerizes; choice of supporting electrolyte (Et4NOTs vs LiClO4) determines whether the naked or Li-chelated radical anion couples.

key_intermediates.name: syn-Z enolate-type anion radical (conformer A, naked)
anti-Z Li-chelated enolate-type anion radical (conformer B)
s-cis anion radical from cis-cinnamoyl substrate 8

electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to the cinnamoyloxazolidinone giving a Z-enolate-type β-carbon radical anion; two such radical anions couple C–C at their β-carbons (Si/Si from naked conformer A → R,R; Re/Re from Li-chelated conformer B → S,S), and the resulting bis-enolate undergoes intramolecular Dieckmann condensation to the cyclopentanone hydrodimer.

probe_reagents.name: cis-cinnamoyloxazolidinone 8 (geometric isomer probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave the same R,R-selective dimer 2a as trans-1a, indicating fast s-cis→s-trans isomerization of the radical anion before coupling.

probe_reagents.name: 3-phenylpropioloyloxazolidinone 9 (alkyne analog)
probe_reagents.observation: Also afforded cyclized dimer 2a (45%, same de) plus non-cyclized diene 10 (15%), supporting in situ generation of 1a-type radical anion.

probe_reagents.name: LiClO4 vs Et4NOTs (counterion probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Et4NOTs gives R,R-dimer (87:13); LiClO4 inverts to S,S-dimer (up to 11:89), consistent with Li-chelated conformation B coupling on the Re face.

probe_reagents.name: ortho/meta/para aryl-substituted 1 (steric/electronic probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Ortho substitution hinders coupling and erodes de (esp. α-naphthyl 1j), consistent with steric control at the coupling face.

probe_reagents.name: cinnamoyloxazolidinethione 11a / thiazolidinethione 11b
probe_reagents.observation: Gave trans-3,4-diphenylcyclopentanone 5 directly via hydrolysis/decarboxylation under electrolysis with similar stereoselectivity.

notes: Authors previously invoked an E-enolate-type anion radical (A') but here revise to Z-enolate conformers A (naked, syn-Z) and B (Li-chelated, anti-Z) based on PM3/AM1 + COSMO semiempirical calculations (Table 3, Scheme 4): naked anion radical favors C (syn-Z, A) while lithiated favors F (anti-Z, B), explaining electrolyte-dependent inversion of stereochemistry. Alternative rationales for diastereoselectivity in the Dieckmann step (cathode-surface templating or water-templating between two radical anions, ref 9) are noted but not adopted here; steric interaction between oxazolidinone substituents is preferred.
